personality_one_liner: A premium, functional, and appetizing interface blending consumer food appeal with high-efficiency internal utility.

spacing.rule: Generous padding around content. Clear grouping. Use iOS-native spacing rhythms and comfortable vertical breathing room.

do's: Use glass on sticky headers over scrolling food menus.
Use glass on the bottom tab bar.
Use a frosted glass overlay for featured food cards when placed over an image.
dont's: Never apply glass to the Cook's KDS cards (needs solid contrast for utility).
Do not blur form fields or body text regions.

screens.name: Login
screens.purpose: Secure authentication acting as a router to the appropriate role-based interface.

states.error: Readable inline error message with retry

screens.layout: Centered form layout. Logo at the top, followed by Employee ID / credentials input, and a sticky bottom CTA for submission. Safe area respected.

screens.name: Employee_Home
screens.purpose: Allow employees to view today's available breakfast/lunch items and add them to cart.

states.empty: Illustration with 'Menu not updated yet' and a refresh CTA.

screens.layout: Top navigation with a prominent search bar and quick cuisine/dietary filters. Large Hero section (Today's Special) using glassmorphism. Below, a 2-column grid of food items (Image, Name, Availability Badge, Price, Add button). Sticky floating CTA to 'View Cart' appears when items are selected.

screens.name: Employee_Order_Tracking
screens.purpose: Track the real-time status of the placed order.
screens.layout: Vertical timeline tracking layout (Pending, Preparing, Ready, Completed). Prominent order number and ETA at the top. Clean, card-based receipt below showing itemized list.

screens.name: Cook_KDS_Dashboard
screens.purpose: A highly functional Kitchen Display System (KDS) for the cook to manage incoming orders in real-time.

states.empty: Illustration: 'No pending orders - Kitchen is clear!'

screens.layout: Back-of-House (BoH) utility focus. Dark mode preferred natively. Top sticky filter bar for status (New, Preparing, Ready). 2-column or 4-ticket grid of Vertical Ticket Cards. Each Ticket Card has a Header (Bold Order #, Timer, Color-coded Tag), Body (Items with bolded modifiers), and Footer (Large 48x48pt 'Ready' / 'Done' single-tap actions).

screens.name: Admin_Dashboard
screens.purpose: High-level overview of orders, statistics, and inventory management.
screens.layout: Single-column vertical stack. Sticky header with Date Range. Top 3-4 KPI Cards (Total Revenue, Orders, Active Users) with deltas. One primary Hero Chart (Order volume over time) optimized for touch. Secondary list at the bottom for 'Most Ordered Items'.

hero_breakfast.alt: A sophisticated breakfast spread featuring coffee, eggs, and bread

empty_state_plate.alt: Empty white plate on dark background

office_catering.alt: A table topped with boxes of sandwiches for office catering

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the color palette. NO BLUES, PURPLES, OR INDIGOS. Use the defined warm Terracotta (#E65100) for primary actions, which simulates appetite and warmth.

For the Cook's KDS Dashboard, implement a Dark-First utility feel. Rely heavily on `surfaceSecondary` and `surfaceTertiary` for ticket cards, and use Space Mono or JetBrains Mono explicitly for timers and order numbers.
The Employee view must feature a 'gradient-to-dark scrim' over hero food images. Layer 1: Image, Layer 2: top-to-bottom transparent to 70% surfaceInverse gradient, Layer 3: Text overlay at bottom.
Use Phosphor icons consistently at 16/20/24 sizes. Keep weight/style uniform (e.g., all filled or all outlined, except active/inactive tab bar).
Any card layout should fill horizontal space gracefully. Never use 48% width orphans; utilize flexWrap with space-between.
Ensure 'Submit Order' or 'Checkout' buttons in Employee flows are sticky at the bottom of the screen, layered over a glassmorphism background to remain accessible during scrolling.
Admin dashboard charts must lead with one Hero Chart that dominates the view, followed by tightly grouped KPIs using the `success` and `error` tokens for deltas.
